Medical & Pharmaceutical Translation

Life-science translation demands absolute accuracy. Our specialized medical linguists provide highly precise translations for clinical trials, regulatory dossiers, and medical devices, strictly adhering to global healthcare standards and compliance requirements.

What We Translate

Clinical trial documentation (protocols, ICFs, CRFs)

Regulatory submissions (EMA, FDA, HSA dossiers)

Pharmaceutical manufacturing documents (SOPs, batch records)

Medical device instructions for use (IFUs)

Patient information leaflets (PILs) & labeling

Pharmacovigilance & safety reports

Why Asiatis for Medical Translation

Global Regulatory Insight

In-depth understanding of European (EMA, MDR) and APAC (HSA) regulatory frameworks and submission requirements.

Specialized Terminology

Vast experience handling precise pharmaceutical and medical terminology across European and Asian language combinations.

Strict Confidentiality

Rigorous data security protocols, secure file transfer, and ironclad NDAs to protect sensitive clinical and intellectual property data.

Back-Translation & Validation

Comprehensive linguistic validation services, including back-translation and reconciliation, essential for clinical trial documents.

Frequently Asked Questions

Yes, we have extensive experience translating Informed Consent Forms (ICFs), Clinical Study Protocols, Case Report Forms (CRFs), and patient questionnaires.
Absolutely. Our global structure allows us to manage large-scale, multilingual regulatory submissions (e.g., EMA dossiers) concurrently across numerous target languages.
We exclusively use translators with medical or pharmaceutical backgrounds. All translations undergo a strict multi-step ISO-aligned quality assurance process involving independent medical reviewers.
We cover all major European and Asian languages, with particular strength in translations between English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Chinese, and Japanese for the life sciencs sector.

Get a Quote for Medical Translation

Contact our Singapore team today to discuss your medical, pharmaceutical, or clinical trial translation requirements.
